---
format: revealjs
---

## Today's Agenda {background-image="libs/Images/background-worldmap4.png" .center}

```{r}
# background-size="1920px 1080px"
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(kableExtra)
```

<br>

::: {.r-fit-text}

**Final Paper Preparations**

- Reviewing international political events

:::

<br>

::: r-stack
Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2024)
:::

::: notes
Prep for Class

1. Review submitted cases and citations

2. IDEA evals day

<br>

By the end of today EVERYONE must have their new international political event chosen.

- Ideally, with time at the end to select the three theories you want to use in the paper!

:::



## IDEA Evaluations {background-image="libs/Images/background-worldmap4.png" .center}

<br>

::: {.r-fit-text}
https://drury.campuslabs.com/eval-home/
:::






## Final Exam (Due May 6, 10am) {background-image="libs/Images/background-blue_triangles2.png" .center}

<br>

Select a **NEW** international political event and make an argument that one of the IR theories / models we've studied in class this term "best" explains the event. 

Your paper must consider and evaluate how **THREE** of the IR theories from class explain your chosen event.

- No double-dipping on realism/liberalism

- At least one theory from the second half of class

- No events used in the prior paper

::: notes

**Questions on the assignment?**

:::



## For Today {background-image="libs/Images/background-blue_triangles2.png" .center}

<br>

1. Find a **NEW** international political event to explain in the final paper (no overlap), and

2. Submit **at least TWO** pieces of evidence that help us understand why this is an international political event.

::: notes

Our aim for today is to make sure everyone has an international political event picked out and ready to analyze in the paper.

<br>

### Did everybody submit their case and evidence?

<br>

*Split class into groups of four*

- Go sit with your group!
:::



## Present Each Event {background-image="libs/Images/background-blue_triangles2.png" .center}

<br>

::: {.r-fit-text}

1. Is this an international political event?

2. Is the evidence high quality?

:::

::: notes

SMALL GROUPS: Discuss each case and give the presenter feedback on these two points.

<br>

### Questions?

- Get to it!

<br>

Alright, let's hear all the cases you've picked!
:::



## For Next Class {background-image="libs/Images/background-blue_triangles2.png" .center}

<br>

1. Submit a description of your chosen event, and

2. Use each theory you have selected to explain your event (2-3 sentences for **EACH** theory)

::: notes
**Questions on the assignment?**

<br>

With the time remaining today everyone needs to select their three theories!

- Use each other as resources, bounce ideas around!

- Go!
:::
